meaning: adj.可调动的 机动的 操纵灵活的
examples: Soviet fighter planes were extremely manoeuvrable.
Each Titan has some basic characteristics which show how fast, manoeuvrable and durable it is.
A brush head support reduces vibration and makes the machine extremely stable yet manoeuvrable.
It is the latest in a series of highly manoeuvrable, versatile, amphibious vehicles manufactured by BAE Systems Haggslunds of Sweden.
The Shannon class is the RNLI's most advanced lifeboat and runs on water jets instead of propellers, making it more manoeuvrable in the water.
